

**Official FAQs** 

Version 1 • Updated: 14 April 2026

![][image1]

Organised by

Google Developer Groups On Campus

Universiti Teknologi Malaysia

**General "Build with AI" & GDG Information**

**What is a "Build with AI" event?**  

Build with AI is a global series of community-led events focused on helping developers learn to create safe, secure, and scalable solutions using Google’s latest AI models and Cloud technology. These events include hands-on workshops, technical sessions, and opportunities to build real projects.

**What will I learn at a Build with AI event?**

* **AI Training:** Hands-on experience with Gemini and other Google AI models.  
* **Core Technologies:** Integration with Firebase, Android, Flutter, and Google Cloud.  
* **Expert Insights:** Best practices and product updates from Google experts and community leaders.

**What are Google Developer Groups (GDG)?**  

GDGs are local, community-run meetups for developers interested in Google technologies. With over 1,000 chapters in 140+ countries, GDGs provide opportunities to:

* **Learn:** Through workshops and talks.  
* **Connect:** Network with developers in your city.  
* **Grow:** Build leadership skills and advance your career.

**Who can attend these events?**  

Build with AI events are open to all developers, whether you are a GDG member or simply looking to expand your network.

**Registration & Eligibility**

**Who can register?**  

The hackathon is open for Public:

* **Student Category:** Ages 16+ in Foundation, Undergraduate, Master’s, or PhD programs.  
* **Open Category:** Ages 16+ beyond university, including professionals and hobbyists.  
* **International Participation:** Students outside Malaysia are welcome; no Malaysian representative is required.  
* **Team Size:** Solo or teams of up to 4 members.

**Do high school students qualify?**  

Yes, you are eligible to participate as long as you are 16 years old and above.

**Do all team members need to register?**  

No, only the Team Leader registers for the team.

**What documents are required?**

* GitHub profile links for **all members** (mandatory).  
* A standard 1-page professional resume from the Team Leader.

**Is there a confirmation letter?**  

No. Once the Team Leader registers and receives the Google Form response sheet, the team is officially eligible.

**Is it compulsory to submit a GitHub profile link for members, even if they don't have one?** 

Yes, it is compulsory. Please create a GitHub account if you don't have one yet.

**What kind of resume does the team leader need to submit?** 

A standard 1-page professional resume (like one you would send for a job application). You can easily use templates from Canva.

**Does the hacking period start immediately?**

The kickoff day was 15 March. You can officially begin working on your projects anytime after this date.

**Can I change my team members after registering?**  

Yes. You can edit your submission directly on the Google form. Please also inform us of the changes by sending an email to [myaifuturehackathon@gmail.com](mailto:myaifuturehackathon@gmail.com).

**If we are a team of international students studying in Malaysia (none of us are Malaysian), which category do we fall under?** 

You will fall under the Student category, but you can also choose to compete in the Open category if you want.

**Tech Stack & Project Building**

**What should I build?**  

Projects must align with the theme AI for Real Impact and use the Google AI Ecosystem Stack (Gemini API, Vertex AI, Firebase Genkit, etc.).

Tracks (Grand Challenges):

* Agrotech & Food Security  
  * *E.g., A web app that integrates Vertex AI for yield forecasting and Firebase Genkit for farmer alerts.*  
* GovTech & Digital Services  
  * *E.g., A chatbot for government services, but instead of just answering queries, it uses Gemini for autonomous execution such as submitting forms or scheduling appointments.*  
* Healthcare & Wellbeing  
  * *E.g., A vital signs monitoring app that collects patient data and uses Gemini AI models to detect anomalies in real time.*  
* Smart Cities & Mobility  
  * *E.g., A public transport app hosted on Google Cloud Run that predicts bus delays and suggests alternative routes.*  
* FinTech & Security  
  * *E.g., A secure budgeting assistant that autonomously executes bill payments and savings transfers using Vertex AI.*

Deployment Requirements:

* All projects must be hosted on **Google Cloud Run.**  
* Final applications must be **web-accessible.**

**Can I use non-Google AI tools?**  

* **Compulsory Tools:** Google AI Studio and Antigravity are the official platforms recommended for building and coding assistance.  
* If you want to integrate AI it needs to be Gemini/Gemma and be built using the Google AI ecosystem stack (Gemini API, Vertex AI, Firebase Genkit, etc.).  
* **Non-AI Tools:** You are free to use any non-AI platforms or frameworks you prefer.

**When are the important dates?**

* **Registration Deadline:** 20th April 2026 at 11:59 PM (MYT).  
* **Submission Deadline:** 21st April 2026 at 11:59 PM (MYT).  
* **Top 50 Finalists Announcement:** 1st May 2026\.  
* **Demo Day (Grand Finale):** 16th – 17th May 2026\.

**Are the sessions virtual or in person?**

* **Workshops:** All technical workshops and speaker sessions are held online.  
* **Final Event:** The Top 50 finalists are required to attend the Final Event on 16-17 May 2026 in person.

**Which requirements should I follow?**  

Always follow the [Official Participant Handbook](https://docs.google.com/document/u/0/d/1ufki6j__FJpbzeVEfMBJvZsFxUiEmywl4oJAiXlZwIQ/edit). Kickoff slides may not be fully updated.

**Does the final product have to be accessible via the web? What about mobile apps?** 

Yes, it must be web-accessible. As per the hackathon's defined tech stack, the final application must be hosted and deployed on Google Cloud Run.

**Can we develop our project to fit multiple tracks?** 

No, you must choose and develop your project within only ONE specific track.

**Are we allowed to use FastAPI for custom backend routing within our Cloud Run deployment?** 

Yes, you can use FastAPI for your custom backend logic within Cloud Run, as long as you fulfill the technical mandate of integrating the mandatory Google AI stack.

**Workshops & Google Cloud Credits**

**Are workshop recordings available?**  

Yes, recordings will be uploaded to YouTube https://www.youtube.com/@gdg\_utm, but live participation is encouraged to claim credits.

**How do I claim credits?**  

Credits are distributed via redemption links provided via email. Registration is required.

**Are credits individual or team-based?**  

Credits are given individually. Each team member can claim them.

**Difference between Workshop vs Hackathon credits?**

* All participants will receive 25 Google Cloud Credits (GCC) during the preliminary round.  
* For each live workshop attended, participants will receive 5 additional GCC.  
* The Top 50 finalists will receive another 25 GCC on Demo Day InfoSession.

**Will certificates be provided?**  

Yes. All participants who make a successful submission (meeting the [successful submission guidelines](#bookmark=id.r0m6f0e7kp4p)) will receive a Certificate of Participation from the organizers.

**How and where can I redeem credits to access the Google Cloud SDK?**

The Google Cloud credits will be distributed via a redemption link during our live workshops.

**I joined the live workshop but didn't fill out the registration form. Can I still claim the credit?**

No. Registration is strictly required for workshops so we can assign the credits to you successfully in the system.

**When is the special meet-up to claim the main Hackathon credits? Will it be online?**

The credit distribution is done virtually. We are currently finalizing the approval process for these credits and will announce the exact date soon.

**Where can I find the feedback form for the workshops?**

The feedback form is shared during the live session itself and is closed shortly after the session ends.

**How many credits will be allocated for the main hackathon, and is it per team or individual?** 

Hackathon credits (25 credits) are given individually to all registered participants. The redemption links will be sent directly to the Team Lead's email.

**Can you announce a monthly schedule for workshops in advance?**

We try our best, but dates can change suddenly as we are coordinating with Google Developer Experts who have busy schedules. New workshops will be announced and added to the Handbook as soon as they are confirmed.

**Submission Guidelines**

**What is required for submission?**

* **Video Demo:** A 3-minute (max) screen recording showing the working prototype.  
* **Final Submission Deadline:** 21 April 2026, 11:59 PM (MYT, GMT+8).

**What is considered a successful submission?**  

A successful submission means your team has:

* Registered before the deadline.  
* Submitted a working prototype deployed on **Google Cloud Run** and **accessible via the web**.  
* Uploaded a **3‑minute (max) video demo** showing the application in action.  
* Followed the official tech stack requirements (integration with Gemini and Google AI ecosystem).

Only teams that meet all these criteria will be evaluated by the judges.

**Can I use Canva instead of Google Slides for my submission pitch deck?** 

Yes, you can.

**If our app has a login dashboard, should we put the username and password in the GitHub README?** 

No, please do not put your login credentials in the GitHub README file. If your application requires a login, you will need to submit the test credentials through a specific Google Form that we will provide to you.

**Where can we find the project submission link?** 

The official submission link has been provided in the \#submission-prep channel on Discord https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSejO3W-SA9Uh4jddYdvcKjQocJWphsXnkj6JzYbbL1bCA\_SoQ/viewform

**Is the submission deadline fixed or will there be an extension?**

The deadline is fixed. There will be no more extensions.

**Demo Day & Finals**

**When is Demo Day?**  

16–17 May 2026\.

**Do all team members need to attend?**

* Mandatory physical presence during your assigned pitching slot.  
* Strongly encouraged to attend opening and closing ceremonies.

**How were the finalists chosen for the Preliminary Round?**  

Finalists were selected based on track representation in each category:

* **Student Category:** Each track has 5 finalist teams. With 5 tracks total, this makes 25 Student Category Finalists.  
* **Open Category:** Each track also has 5 finalist teams. With 5 tracks total, this makes 25 Open Category Finalists.

In total, 50 teams (25 Student \+ 25 Open) were chosen as finalists.

**Does every team member need to speak during the pitch?**

There is no strict rule. One presenter is fine, though having full team participation makes the pitch more engaging and shows great teamwork.

**Will travel grants be provided?**  

Organizers are working to secure travel grants and accommodations, but they are not guaranteed.

**Where will the finalist event/Demo Day be held? UTM KL or UTM Skudai?**

It will be held physically at UTM Skudai.

**If an international team makes it to the finals but cannot attend offline due to budget restrictions, can they present online?** 

Unfortunately, no. The final pitching is strictly an offline (physical) event.

**Are prizes awarded?**  

Yes, six national titles across Student and Open categories:

| Prize Rank | Student Category | Open Category |
| :---: | :---: | :---: |
| **1st Place** | **RM 2,500National Student Champion** | **RM 5,000National Open Champion** |
| **2nd Place** | **RM 1,500Student 1st Runner-Up** | **RM 3,500Open 1st Runner-Up** |
| **3rd Place** | **RM 500Student 2st Runner-Up** | **RM 2,000Open 2st Runner-Up** |

### All participants who submit a project will receive a Certificate of Participation.

###   

### **Logistics & Support**

### **Is registration required?**  

### Yes, registration is free. Team compositions are fixed after 20 April 2026\.

### **Who organizes the Project 2030: MyAI Future Hackathon?**

### This hackathon is community-run by GDG On Campus UTM, with Google providing technical support and mentors.

### **How do I stay informed?**

You may use the following platforms to receive updates or contact us for support.
